tab.personas: Who Vinkona is when speaking. Each persona is a system prompt plus a voice; the active one shapes every reply. Edits apply on the next conversation turn — no restart. Personas live in the active profile, so switching profiles switches the persona set too.
tab.profiles: Complete separate identities: each profile has its own memory database, personas, and settings overrides. 'Fork' snapshots the current profile into a copy you can experiment on. The Memory and Personas tabs always act on the ACTIVE profile.
tab.models: Which GGUF each language-model tier loads, on which GPU, with which llama.cpp settings. fast_lm answers in real time; big_lm reasons in the background; embed_lm powers memory recall; tts_lm is the Orpheus voice backbone (only used by the orpheus_gguf engine). Changes need a service restart (Services tab).
tab.tuning: The most-touched voice-loop knobs, surfaced from Settings: microphone gain, voice-activity thresholds, barge-in sensitivity, ASR confidence gates. Same values as Settings — this is just the short list.
tab.services: Live status and logs for every process in the stack, with restart buttons. A restart request is written to logs/control/ and the monitor window in tmux performs it — so it works even though this UI runs in the container.
tab.live: The live trace: every turn's pipeline events (VAD, ASR text, LM calls, tool calls, TTS timing) as they happen. The place to look when something sounds wrong — you can see exactly what was heard and what each stage did with it.
tab.memory: Everything Vinkona remembers about you: browsable, searchable, editable, deletable. Entries carry priority, triggers, and tags that shape when they're recalled. Quarantined entries were flagged by the safety layer and never reach the model until you approve them.
tab.self: Vinkona's model of itself and of you: communication preferences, corrections you've given, domain fluency estimates. Built passively from conversations; informs retrieval ranking and how answers are phrased.
tab.research: The autonomous research worker: what it's investigating during idle time, its sources, and the export that drops solved topics into the knowledge host. The header's pause button (and Settings → research → idle → quiet_hours) stops all of this to free the LMs.
tab.plans: Learning plans the research worker is executing: each topic breaks into research questions (answered autonomously) and ask-user questions (awaiting you). Reopen a plan to make it re-research with fresh sources.
tab.settings: Every option in config.json, rendered as a form — new options appear automatically. Hover a field's ? for what it does; that text comes from the option's comment in config.py, so it is always current. Save writes config.json; most changes need a restart of the affected service (Services tab).
